title: NCEM Drop - Tissue Dounce Homogenisation (TM-014)
steps: Adsorb 10 µL sample to grid 00:10:00 , inspect to ensure sample does not dry out.
Drain excess sample from grid using filter paper, leave wet.
Stain nano-W Contributed by users Catalog ##2018-5ML 00:01:00
This method is often used for scabs, warts, or chunks of tissue such as liver. Place ~ 5mm x 5mm piece of scab or tissue into glass homogeniser tube.
Add equal volume TC water to tube, homogenise(20 strokes) inside Class II BSC cabinet.
Insert teflon plunger into cordless drill, set drill on max speed.
Clarify resultant solution at 13000 rpm, °C, in Eppendorf centrifuge for 00:01:00
Stand for 00:05:00 to permit viruses to diffuse back into solution, from the debris, and provide an interface for the sampling of membrane associated viruses. Use supernatant for sample below.
creator: sandra.crameri
